cluster_explanations.candidate_name: high-contrast, dynamic visual elements with motion or texture variation
cluster_explanations.explanation: The samples consistently feature regions with high visual salience: bright colors (green, blue, red), sharp edges, or textured surfaces (e.g., fabric, water, book spines). Many show motion or changes across frames (e.g., hands moving, fire flickering, water splashing), which naturally attract and maintain visual attention. The eye is drawn to these areas because they provide strong visual contrast, motion cues, or detailed patterns that stand out against the background or surrounding static elements.

cluster_explanations.candidate_name: crowd scenes with dynamic human activity
cluster_explanations.explanation: All samples depict human crowds, often in motion or engaged in expressive actions (clapping, cheering, gesturing). The visual pattern is characterized by high human density, varied clothing colors, and dynamic movement, which naturally captures attention due to the social and emotional cues conveyed by the crowd. Eye attention is fixated on these patches because they represent the most salient and behaviorally rich regions in the scene, contrasting with static or less interactive backgrounds.

cluster_explanations.candidate_name: High-contrast, low-resolution, or motion-blurred visual fragments
cluster_explanations.explanation: All 10 examples are visually fragmented, low-resolution, or motion-blurred patches. They lack coherent structure or context, appearing as isolated, noisy, or indistinct visual elements. Eye attention is fixated on these patches because they are the only salient, albeit ambiguous, stimuli in the visual field — they stand out due to their high contrast, motion artifacts, or abrupt changes compared to the surrounding uniform or static background. The brain prioritizes processing these anomalous or dynamic regions, even if they are not meaningful.

cluster_explanations.candidate_name: high-contrast motion edges and blurred dynamic scenes
cluster_explanations.explanation: The samples consistently feature areas with high contrast, sharp edges, or rapid motion blur, which naturally attract visual attention. These include: 1) sharp boundaries between colors (e.g., blue/red surfaces, snowboard edges); 2) motion-blurred objects (e.g., blue smoke, people in motion); 3) high-contrast static structures (e.g., power lines, scaffolding). These elements stand out due to their visual salience, creating strong edges or motion cues that the visual system prioritizes for processing.

cluster_explanations.candidate_name: High-contrast, salient visual elements with strong color or motion cues
cluster_explanations.explanation: All 10 examples feature regions that are visually striking due to high contrast, saturated colors, or dynamic motion. cluster_explanations.candidate_name: Highly detailed close-ups of hands interacting with objects or textures
cluster_explanations.explanation: All 10 examples feature extreme close-ups focused on hands or fingers engaging with items (e.g., holding objects, applying nail polish, manipulating fabric, or gripping tools). The visual pattern is characterized by high detail, texture, and motion blur, which naturally draws attention due to the salience of human interaction and fine-grained visual cues. Eye attention is fixated on these patches because they represent dynamic, tactile, and often personally relevant actions that are visually rich and behaviorally significant.
